name: input-mask
title: Input Mask
description: An Input that guides entry of a fixed-format value (phone, SSN, ZIP, ZIP+4, date MM/DD/YYYY). Sets inputMode, pattern, and maxLength for the mask and, by default, inserts separators as the user types — without blocking valid keystrokes, hijacking paste, or trapping the caret. A `secure` prop obscures sensitive values (e.g. an SSN) as password bullets with a keyboard-operable reveal toggle, while masking still applies. Presets plus custom mask templates. The mask is a client-side guide; authoritative validation belongs on the server.
useWhen: Collecting a fixed-format, known-length value (US phone, SSN, ZIP or ZIP+4, MM/DD/YYYY date)
The separators are predictable and the same for every valid entry
A visible format guide helps users enter and self-correct the value
avoidWhen: Free text or any value whose length or shape varies (use a plain Input)
Values that need real validation as the source of truth (validate on the server; the mask only guides)
International formats where the pattern differs by locale (offer a plain Input or locale-specific field)
A whole date users think of as parts (consider the memorable-date pattern)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the control; Shift+Tab moves focus away backwards
Printable keys insert characters; separators are added automatically and never block a valid keystroke
Backspace and Delete remove characters freely — deleting a digit also drops its separators, so the caret is never trapped
Paste is accepted and re-masked; the value can always be cleared and corrected
When secure, Tab reaches the reveal toggle; Enter or Space shows or hides the value
A disabled control is removed from the tab order
